text: Struggle to apply AI. Through its investors, the laboratory has been able to build enormous development capacity, both in terms of computing power and scarce AI skills. This stands in stark contrast to the struggle of the average company to embed such technology in their daily practice. In 2020, only 13 per cent of Dutch companies used one or more AI technologies, according to figures from [CBS](https://opendata.cbs.nl/#/CBS/nl/dataset/84830NED/barh?ts=1670493464021).

metadata.tags: Column, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Digitalisering, AI
metadata.parent_text: Struggle to apply AI. Through its investors, the laboratory has been able to build enormous development capacity, both in terms of computing power and scarce AI skills. This stands in stark contrast to the struggle of the average company to embed such technology in their daily practice. In 2020, only 13 per cent of Dutch companies used one or more AI technologies, according to figures from [CBS](https://opendata.cbs.nl/#/CBS/nl/dataset/84830NED/barh?ts=1670493464021).
metadata.source_title: Spectacular chatbot symbol of AI divide
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: ChatGPT can handle the most diverse requests. Meanwhile, companies struggle to put AI into practice. Read more.

id: Advertentiemodel onder druk door nieuwe Europese wetgeving__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0075
text: Although it is not yet possible to determine exactly how many companies in the Netherlands will be affected, it is already clear that many websites depend on advertising revenue. Of the ten million most-visited websites worldwide, almost [47 per cent](https://w3techs.com/technologies/history_overview/advertising/all "Historical trends in the usage statistics of advertising networks for websites") use an ‘ad network’. This is a technological solution that aggregates online ad space from publishers and offers it to advertisers. The extent to which these websites will still be able to generate revenue through personalised advertising is likely to decline sharply. The new process requirements make it easier for internet users to refuse tracking. The consequences of redesigning such a process should not be underestimated.

metadata.tags: Analyse, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Retail, Leisure, Digitalisering, Wet- en regelgeving
metadata.chunk_id: Advertentiemodel onder druk door nieuwe Europese wetgeving__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0075

Of the ten million most-visited websites worldwide, almost [47 per cent](https://w3techs.com/technologies/history_overview/advertising/all "Historical trends in the usage statistics of advertising networks for websites") use an ‘ad network’. This is a technological solution that aggregates online ad space from publishers and offers it to advertisers. The extent to which these websites will still be able to generate revenue through personalised advertising is likely to decline sharply. The new process requirements make it easier for internet users to refuse tracking. The consequences of redesigning such a process should not be underestimated.
metadata.source_slug: Advertentiemodel onder druk door nieuwe Europese wetgeving
metadata.description: This article explains how the Digital Services Act will make ‘tracking’ individuals for advertising purposes more difficult and lists alternatives.
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.source_title: Advertising model under pressure due to new European legislation

id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0034
text: In 2021, people spent on average about [seven hours](https://datareportal.com/reports/digital-2022-october-global-statshot "DIGITAL 2022: OCTOBER GLOBAL STATSHOT REPORT") a day in online channels. Advertisers therefore spend billions a year to show their message to website visitors and app users. This market is growing fast; in 2021 this amount in Europe had risen to [92 billion euros](https://iabeurope.eu/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/adex-2021-advertising-report.pdf "DIGITAL ADVERTISING IN EUROPE") – an increase of 30.5 per cent compared with the previous year. In the Netherlands, digital ad spend exceeded advertising spend in traditional media for the first time in 2016 and by now the digital ad spend is already more than twice as large. For the owners of websites, apps and other content – also known as ‘publishers’ – the internet offers opportunities to monetise their ad space. For example, almost half of the largest websites use a so-called ad network. This is a technological solution that collects online ad space from publishers and offers it to advertisers. Besides advertisers and publishers, there are numerous intermediaries and other service providers active in the online advertising market. Advertisers are supported by media, marketing and advertising agencies, which help determine the most effective channels for online ad campaigns and often also play a role in the associated purchase of ad space. There are also numerous so-called AdTech players, which use technological solutions to facilitate the buying and selling of online ad space. Personalised advertising as standard practice.

metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Retail, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.chunk_id: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0034
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.source_title: Privacy protection puts online advertising at a turning point
metadata.description: The online advertising market is exploring alternatives to personalised ads due to privacy legislation. Read more.
metadata.source_slug: Bescherming privacy zet online adverteren op keerpunt

id: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0033
text: Digital ad spend drives media sector. Developments in the advertising market are a good barometer for developments within the media sector. In 2023, around € 5.2 billion was spent on advertising in the Netherlands, of which 71 per cent was digital advertising, that is € 3.7 billion, according to the VIA/Deloitte Digital Advertising Spend report for 2023 (see chart). Revenue growth for digital advertising was 5 per cent compared with 2022. Compared with an average annual growth rate of 11 per cent in the ten years before, this was relatively low. In 2024, ABN AMRO expects 9 per cent revenue growth for digital advertising, more in line with the historical average. Growth rates of 11 per cent or more from the past are no longer to be expected because digitalisation has now progressed further and some submarkets are saturated. Paid search, for example, is growing somewhat more slowly, while online video, social media and digital audio are growing somewhat faster.

metadata.source_slug: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen
metadata.source_title: Digital media grow through transformation and new channels
metadata.description: Digital media are growing strongly through transformation and new channels, while traditional media are shrinking. Trends and outlook. Read more.
metadata.author: Mario Bersem
metadata.chunk_id: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0033
